principle: One governed task has five role-oriented views over the same immutable task and event lineage.

business.purpose: Explain why the task exists and what business value is expected.

outcome.purpose: Show what was produced, verified, adopted, rejected, or superseded.

failure.purpose: Describe the failed stage, impact, retry eligibility, and preserved partial work.

core_issue.purpose: Separate symptoms from the best supported root cause and unresolved unknowns.

handling.purpose: State who owns the next action, approval route, deadline, containment, and recovery.

invariants: Views reference one task_id and trace_id.
Failure does not erase partial outcomes.
A symptom cannot be promoted to root cause without evidence.
Every open failure or core issue must have a handling owner or be explicitly frozen.
A correction appends a new event and does not overwrite prior history.
